---
title: "Analyzing Music Data"
author: "Ishika Ravat"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)

billboard |> 
  select(artist, track, date.entered, wk1:wk4)
```

```{r}
ggplot(billboard, aes(x = wk6)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Week 6 Rankings",
    subtitle = paste0("Number of values: ", sum(!is.na(billboard$wk6))),
    x = "Week 6 Rank",
    y = "Count"
  )
```

```{r}
billboard |> 
  select(wk1, wk4, wk10, wk20, wk40, wk76) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(), 
    names_to = "week", 
    values_to = "rank"
  ) |> 
  mutate(week = factor(week, levels = c("wk1", "wk4", "wk10", "wk20", "wk40", "wk76"))) |> 
  group_by(week) |> 
  summarise(
    present = sum(!is.na(rank)),
    missing = sum(is.na(rank))
  )
```

```{r}
billboard |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("wk"), 
    names_to = "week", 
    names_prefix = "wk", 
    values_to = "rank", 
    values_drop_na = FALSE
  ) |> 
  mutate(week = as.integer(week)) |> 
  group_by(artist, track) |> 
  summarise(
    re_entered = any(is.na(rank) & week < max(week[!is.na(rank)])), 
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |> 
  count(re_entered)
```

```{r}
billboard_long <- billboard |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("wk"), 
    names_to = "week", 
    names_prefix = "wk", 
    values_to = "rank", 
    values_drop_na = TRUE
  ) |> 
  mutate(week = as.integer(week))

ggplot(billboard_long, aes(x = week, y = rank, group = interaction(artist, track))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.2, color = "steelblue") +
  scale_y_reverse() +
  labs(
    title = "Song Rankings Over Time",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Rank (Reversed)"
  )
```

```{r}
# Create song-level summary
song_summary <- billboard_long |> 
  group_by(artist, track) |> 
  summarise(
    first_rank = rank[week == min(week)],
    best_rank = min(rank),
    first_week_at_best = min(week[rank == best_rank]),
    total_weeks = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Display a preview of the song summary
head(song_summary, 10)

# Identify three notable songs
fastest_no1 <- song_summary |> 
  filter(best_rank == 1) |> 
  slice_min(first_week_at_best, n = 1) |> 
  mutate(category = "Fastest to reach #1")

slowest_no1 <- song_summary |> 
  filter(best_rank == 1) |> 
  slice_max(first_week_at_best, n = 1) |> 
  mutate(category = "Slowest to reach #1")

longest_top10 <- song_summary |> 
  filter(best_rank <= 10) |> 
  slice_max(total_weeks, n = 1) |> 
  mutate(category = "Top-10 with longest run")

# Combine and print the results
bind_rows(fastest_no1, slowest_no1, longest_top10) |> 
  select(category, artist, track, first_rank, best_rank, first_week_at_best, total_weeks)
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
# Filter for songs that reached the top 10
top10_songs <- billboard_long |> 
  group_by(artist, track) |> 
  filter(min(rank) <= 10) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  mutate(
    highlight = case_when(
      artist == "Madonna" & track == "Music" ~ "Madonna - Music (Fastest to #1)",
      artist == "Lonestar" & track == "Amazed" ~ "Lonestar - Amazed (Slowest to #1)",
      artist == "Creed" & track == "Higher" ~ "Creed - Higher (Longest run)",
      TRUE ~ "Other Top 10"
    )
  )

# Plot top-10 songs, highlighting the three notable ones
ggplot() +
  geom_line(
    data = filter(top10_songs, highlight == "Other Top 10"),
    aes(x = week, y = rank, group = interaction(artist, track), color = highlight),
    alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = filter(top10_songs, highlight != "Other Top 10"),
    aes(x = week, y = rank, group = interaction(artist, track), color = highlight),
    alpha = 1, linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  scale_y_reverse() +
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "Notable Songs",
    values = c(
      "Madonna - Music (Fastest to #1)" = "#E41A1C",
      "Lonestar - Amazed (Slowest to #1)" = "#377EB8",
      "Creed - Higher (Longest run)" = "#4DAF4C",
      "Other Top 10" = "gray85"
    ),
    breaks = c(
      "Madonna - Music (Fastest to #1)",
      "Lonestar - Amazed (Slowest to #1)",
      "Creed - Higher (Longest run)"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 Songs: Highlighted Notable Runs",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Rank (Reversed)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
